_about: Gold set for the thesis eval. Each item has a question and verified expectations. The scorer runs the pipeline and grades against these. 'expected_tables' drives the table-recall metric (the 'did it miss a table?' worry). 'expected_value' (optional) is checked against the executed SQL's result for numeric correctness. 'expect_chart' checks a valid chart was produced. Build this against YOUR demo DB; the entries below assume a sales/spendings/produced_items/returns schema.

items.question: Show total revenue per month for 2024 as a line chart.
What was net revenue in Q4?
items.notes: TABLE-RECALL TRAP: net revenue needs the returns table. Without the glossary or good retrieval, a model answers gross revenue from sales alone and silently misses 'returns'. This item is the core test of the scaling/recall claim.

items.question: Why were Q3 sales weak?
items.notes: Routes to BOTH arms: numbers (sales/production dip) + documents (the cause). Tests the router + doc-RAG.

items.question: What was our gross margin by quarter?
items.notes: Defined metric (gross_margin). With glossary the formula is given; without it the model must infer revenue-vs-cost itself.
